title: Double typhoon approaches Japan, triggering warnings of torrential rain and transport disruptions
summary: Typhoons 7 and 8 are expected to impact the Kanto area, causing train suspensions and the risk of landslides.

title: Japan draws with Sweden and advances to the World Cup round of 16 as second in Group F
summary: The Japanese national team secured its place in the next round after a goalless draw against Sweden, with a standout performance from goalkeeper Suzuki.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 3,500 points due to massive sell-off of tech stocks
summary: The Tokyo stock market suffered heavy losses dragged down by the collapse of artificial intelligence stocks.

title: ANA apologizes for glitches in new reservation system and government orders investigation
summary: Technical problems in the reservation system update caused inconvenience to passengers; the airline plans improvements.

title: Japan prepares to face Brazil in the World Cup round of 16
summary: The match against the five-time world champions is scheduled for July 30 and a high-intensity duel is expected.

title: Japanese Defense Ministry confirms USB malware infection in the Army
summary: Minister Koizumi assured that the systems were not affected and that cybersecurity measures will be strengthened.

title: Tension grows in the Strait of Hormuz with ship attacks and toll disputes
summary: Iran attacked a cargo ship in the strait, while the United States and Gulf countries reject navigation fees.

title: Actor Murakami Nijiro sent to prosecutors for alleged assault on his partner
summary: He is accused of causing serious injuries to a woman with whom he had a romantic relationship.

title: Chief Cabinet Secretary's deputy replaced over inappropriate trip scandal
summary: Mogi was replaced after it was revealed he used public funds for a trip with an acquaintance.

title: Veterans and young players of the Japanese national team make their mark at the World Cup
summary: Defender Yuto Nagatomo and wing-back Yusei Sugawara were key pieces in the team's scheme.

title: Japan advances regulation of social media for minors with risk assessments
summary: The government proposes requiring SNS operators to conduct risk assessments to protect children.

title: Nissan recalls more than 600,000 vehicles for unspecified problems
summary: The Note, Note Aura, and X-Trail models are called for inspection in Japan.

title: Magnitude 5.8 earthquake shakes Chiba and Ibaraki prefectures without tsunami warning
summary: The quake reached an intensity 4 on the Japanese seismic scale and no major damage was reported.

title: Japan's minimum wage approaches 1,500 yen per hour amid intense negotiations
summary: The government and social partners are discussing a significant increase to meet the 1,500 yen target.

title: Devastating earthquake in Venezuela leaves 235 dead and more than 4,300 injured
summary: The large-magnitude quake caused havoc and 40,000 people are listed as missing.

title: Justice minister apologizes for using mobile phone during parliamentary interpellation
summary: Hiraguchi admitted to handling his phone in the Senate plenary and offered apologies.

title: Fuji Television and SBI Holdings explore alliance for content production and distribution
summary: Both companies are evaluating collaboration on television programs, anime, and international expansion.

title: Honda holds shareholders meeting and approves re-election of directors after losses in electric vehicles
summary: The automaker faced criticism for its losses in the EV business and modified its strategy.

title: Education Ministry requests review of safety measures in schools after fire in Tokyo
summary: The fire at a primary school in Kita-ku prompted the demand to evaluate evacuation protocols.

title: Japan approves law transforming the Air Force into the Air and Space Self-Defense Force
summary: The new designation includes the surveillance of outer space among the force's missions.
